rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Washington Group International Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the statute of limitations issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Under the analysis of Uram v. Abex Corporation (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1425, plaintiff submitted evidence creating a triable issue. Plaintiff¿s various depositions and declaration and Dr. Raybin¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff¿s breathing problems were caused by asbestos exposure. Further, plaintiff submitted evidence of a triable issue as to whether he knew or in the exercise of reasonable diligence that he should have known the causes or contributing factors of his disability. Since the ¿uncontradicted facts¿ do not have only one legitimate inference, summary judgment is not proper. (Jolly v. Eli Lilly and Company (1988) 44 Cal.3d 1103.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Beazer East, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff cannot prevail under his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action because it did not demonstrate that this cause of action fails even if plaintiff cannot establish liability under either a retained control theory or under a concealed condition theory. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages if off calendar as plaintiff has dismissed such claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Washington Group International Inc.¿s joinder in motions for summary judgment/adjudication filed by Johnson Controls, Inc., Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc. and Safeway, Inc. is denied. Defendant¿s so-called joinder failed to include a separate statement in violation of C.C.P. 437c(b)(1). Defendant cannot join another party¿s summary motion. (Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627.) Defendant¿s joinder is also denied as untimely for failing to provide sufficient notice required by C.C.P. 437c(a). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant George F. Schuler, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s motion are overruled. The grounds upon which defendant moved for summary judgment were made clear in defendant¿s supporting papers. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Blue Diamond Growers¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense would apply to bar plaintiff¿s premises owner/contractor liability claim based on failure to warn. The court cannot summarily adjudicate plaintiff¿s claims based on failure to warn because defendant failed to specify that it sought adjudication of this issue of duty in its Notice of Motion. Therefore defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense is a complete bar to plaintiff¿s claims that negates any duty defendant may have owed to Mr. Hanson. Defendant¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages if off calendar as plaintiff has dismissed such claim. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD L. LEE VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 3, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendants Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendants failed to sustain their initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendants failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claim for punitive damages. Defendants failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendants acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendants¿ special interrogatories preclude defendants from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
